Color Picker

Pick any color and get its HEX, RGB, HSL, and CMYK values instantly. Copy any format with one click.

HSLhsl(210, 100%, 45%)
Need to make an image file smaller?Try Image Compressor

Color codes are the language of digital design. Every color you see in a web page, app interface, or digital illustration is defined by one of several color model representations, HEX for web use, RGB for screen display, HSL for human-readable hue and lightness adjustments, or CMYK for print production. Translating between these formats manually requires math. This tool gives you an interactive color picker where you can select any color visually and immediately see all its code representations, copy the format you need with a single click.

How to use

  1. Select a color. Click anywhere on the color spectrum to select a hue, then adjust the saturation and brightness using the gradient picker. The selected color appears as a preview swatch alongside all its code values.
  2. Fine-tune with the hue slider. Use the hue bar at the bottom of the picker to move through the full color wheel. Combine hue adjustments with the gradient position to reach any specific color.
  3. Enter a known code. If you already have a HEX or RGB value, type it directly into the input field to see the color preview and get all other format representations automatically.
  4. Copy the value you need. Click the copy icon next to any format, HEX, RGB, HSL, or CMYK, to copy that specific representation to your clipboard, ready to paste into your code editor or design tool.

Tips for best results

  • Use HSL for systematic color variations. HSL (Hue, Saturation, Lightness) makes it easy to create consistent color variants, adjust only the Lightness to get lighter or darker versions of the same hue, or adjust Saturation to mute or intensify without changing the underlying color. CSS supports HSL natively.
  • Check contrast ratios for accessibility. After picking a foreground and background color pair, verify the contrast ratio meets WCAG AA (4.5:1 for normal text) or AAA (7:1) standards before using them in a UI. Dark text on light backgrounds and vice versa are the most accessible combinations.
  • Extract colors from images with the palette tool. If you want to match colors from an existing image or photo, use the color palette generator to extract the dominant colors as HEX values, then look up those exact codes here to get all format representations.
  • Use HEX for web, CMYK for print. Web and screen design uses HEX or RGB. Print design uses CMYK. The values can differ significantly for highly saturated colors because RGB and CMYK have different gamuts, bright screen reds may not reproduce accurately in CMYK print.

Why use PixMidas

  • All formats at once. HEX, RGB, HSL, and CMYK values are all shown simultaneously for every selected color. No need to use multiple converter tools or perform manual calculations.
  • 100% private. The color picker runs entirely in your browser with no data transmission of any kind, though colors aren't sensitive data, no unnecessary network requests are made.
  • No account needed. Free, instant, unlimited color lookups. Works in all modern browsers.

Frequently asked questions

What is the difference between HEX, RGB, and HSL?

HEX is a six-character string (e.g., #3a86ff) representing red, green, and blue channel values in hexadecimal. RGB uses three decimal values (e.g., rgb(58, 134, 255)) representing the same channels. HSL uses three values: Hue (0–360 degrees on the color wheel), Saturation (0–100%), and Lightness (0–100%). All three can represent the same color, HEX and RGB are interchangeable; HSL is a different mathematical model that's often easier to reason about for color adjustments.

What is CMYK and when do I use it?

CMYK stands for Cyan, Magenta, Yellow, and Key (Black), the four ink colors used in color printing. It's a subtractive color model where inks are mixed to absorb light. Web and screen designs use RGB (an additive model). When preparing files for print, your design tool converts RGB colors to CMYK. The conversion can shift some colors significantly, especially bright saturated colors outside the CMYK gamut. Check CMYK values when preparing files for professional printing.

Why does my HEX value look different in print?

Screen colors (RGB) and print colors (CMYK) have different gamuts, the range of colors each medium can reproduce. Bright, highly saturated screen colors like neon greens, electric blues, and vivid oranges often fall outside the CMYK gamut and are approximated (usually appearing duller) when printed. This is a fundamental limitation of ink-on-paper printing. Professional print work uses Pantone spot colors for brand-critical colors that need to match precisely in print.

How do I find the HEX code of a color on my screen?

The best approach is to use your browser's DevTools color picker, which can sample any pixel on screen. In Chrome DevTools, click on any color value in the Styles panel to open the picker, then use the eyedropper to sample any screen pixel. For images specifically, the color palette generator extracts dominant colors directly from uploaded images.

What is the HEX code for common web colors?

Pure red: #ff0000. Pure green: #00ff00. Pure blue: #0000ff. White: #ffffff. Black: #000000. The named CSS colors (like "tomato", "coral", "steelblue") have specific HEX values defined in the CSS specification. Paste any named CSS color into the HEX input field and the picker will display the exact swatch and all format values.

Can I pick a color from an image?

The color picker selects colors from the spectrum gradient. To sample colors from an existing image, use the color palette generator which extracts the dominant colors from any uploaded photo or graphic with their HEX and RGB values. Paste those HEX values into this picker to get HSL and CMYK representations.

Privacy Note & Disclaimer

PixMidas is designed to process files directly in your browser whenever possible, and we do not intentionally store or inspect your files. However, no online service can guarantee absolute security, so please avoid highly sensitive data. By using PixMidas, you acknowledge that files are processed at your own discretion and risk.
